articleTitle: Effect of chlorhexidine pretreatment on clinical performance of adhesive restorations: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
takeaways: Chlorhexidine pretreatment before dental fillings can inhibit matrix metalloproteinases (MMPs), enzymes that break down the bond between tooth and filling material
The hybrid layer - the critical interface where dental adhesive bonds to tooth structure - deteriorates over time through both water damage and enzymatic breakdown
Chlorhexidine digluconate (CHX) application may extend the lifespan of dental restorations by preserving the adhesive bond integrity
Resin-bonded dental restorations face long-term stability challenges that chlorhexidine pretreatment could potentially address
